title: TLS, Authentication, Virtual Hosts, Authorization, and Secrets · Type Recall
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs tls, authentication, virtual hosts, authorization, and secrets from bounded publish, route, delivery, acknowledgement, and recovery evidence, challenges one misconception, and revises the messaging identity, transport, and least-privilege matrix. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for messaging identity, transport, and least-privilege matrix after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal…
objectives: Use TLS with trusted CA, hostname and SNI verification, minimum protocol policy, client identity where required, and tested certificate rotation.
Choose generated credentials, x509 EXTERNAL, or OAuth 2 identity with explicit broker, client, token, renewal, and revocation support.
Grant minimum configure, write, and read regex permissions per virtual host and separately evaluate topic authorization.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Amqps Identity Verification · changed case · Review Amqps Identity Verifica…
steps.instruction:
review case — A review messaging team handles case 4380 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to gate verified TLS and vhost access while preserving separate configure, write, and read permissions; show recovery after the changed failure is removed; then remove the dead-letter binding and expose the resulting retention risk. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Use TLS with trusted CA, hostname and SNI verification, minimum protocol policy, client identity where required, and tested certificate rotation.
